Probabilistic NO3-N hardness-class weights.

Source: R/paf.R
dot-no3_weights.Rd

The three nitrate SSDs (soft / moderate / hard) have abrupt class boundaries at 30 and 150 mg/L CaCO3. Rather than snap a sample to one class, treat the true hardness as log-normal around the measured value (CV = hardness_cv) and weight the classes by the probability mass falling in each band. This smooths the PAF across boundaries without biologically interpolating between the distinct SSDs.

Usage

.no3_weights(hardness_mg_L, hardness_cv = 0.05)

Arguments

hardness_mg_L

Numeric. Measured hardness in mg/L as CaCO3.

hardness_cv

Numeric. CV of the hardness measurement (default 0.05).

Value

Named numeric vector c(soft, mod, hard) summing to 1, or all-NA if hardness_mg_L is missing/non-positive.

On this page
